productContext.goal: Build a static and semi-interactive desktop prototype that demonstrates Lobster's agent-assisted group-chat resonance without integrating the real Tencent client or real-time backend services.
nonGoals: Direct QQ or Tencent production API integration
Real identity, OAuth, or account linking
Full backend persistence for production use
True multi-user synchronization in the current phase

principles: Current phase is a single-user narrative prototype with static messages and deterministic interactions.
Frontend uses local JSON or in-memory mock data in the current phase.
Frontend will talk only to Lobster BFF in the future LLM-enhanced phase, never directly to Aliyun LLM.
LLM handles intent understanding, slot extraction, card copy generation, and tool routing suggestions only after phase upgrade.
Business state mutations are deterministic operations, not LLM outputs.
Mock Tencent ecosystem actions are represented as adapters with simulated responses.
All agent interventions are opt-in and visible as explicit system artifacts.

layers.name: Mock Desktop Client
responsibilities: Display static conversations, message flow, built-in cards, and scenario controls

layers.name: Local Mock Data Layer
responsibilities: Provide static conversations, messages, and scenario fixtures
Provide the three built-in card templates
Drive deterministic state transitions for demo actions
Persist temporary local UI state if needed

layers.name: Aliyun LLM Adapter
responsibilities: Call the Aliyun Open Platform model endpoint
Parse and validate structured JSON outputs
Retry or fall back to templates on malformed model responses
technology: Aliyun Open Platform SDK or HTTPS adapter

layers.name: Mock Fulfillment Adapters
responsibilities: Provide deterministic demo responses for card actions

passiveIntercept: User views or triggers a predefined scenario message in the mock chat client.
Client local rule layer matches one of the three built-in PRD scenarios.
Client shows a private suggestion affordance only to the current user.
After confirmation, the corresponding built-in card is inserted into the public message flow.
Further interactions mutate local card state deterministically.
proactiveSummon: User opens the agent entry point explicitly from the chat UI.
Client offers one of the three built-in card templates based on the chosen scenario.
Client inserts the card into MessageFlow or the reserved agent slot.
No LLM call occurs in the current phase.
cardAction: User clicks a card action such as join, open spoiler thread, or mark interest.
Client applies a deterministic local state transition.
Client updates the card visual state and optional local event log.
Future phases may route the same action through a local BFF and mock fulfillment adapters.

allowed: Action recommendation and internal tool routing suggestions

storageModel.recommendedPhase1: Local HTTP mock service plus JSON-backed state
storageModel.recommendedPhase2: SQLite or lightweight document store for scenario state

openQuestions: Whether passive intercept should run only on selected messages or also on outgoing draft text in the prototype.
Whether scenario state should reset per app launch or persist across sessions.
How much conversation context should be exposed to the Aliyun adapter in phase 1.
Whether fictional senders should be modeled only as message metadata or as lightweight personas.
